name: Claude Thornhill
abstract: Claude Thornhill (August 10, 1908 – July 1, 1965) was an American pianist, arranger, composer, and bandleader. He composed the jazz and pop standards "Snowfall" and "I Wish I Had You".

about_quotes.speaker: John Best
about_quotes.evidence: he said Davy Tough, Max Kaminsky, Claude Thornhill and Sam Donahue. And it sounded like a good group, which it was... So I actually had three ways... So I was fortunate enough to get with that band
about_quotes.doc_title: John Best interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: the experience with Ray Noble... he and Claude Thornhill didn't get on too well... we had these sort of plastic-paper combination like music cases... Ray and his orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Rusty Dedrick
about_quotes.evidence: We left Red Norvo’s band to go with Claude Thornhill’s band. ... But still Claude gave me a free hand, and he paid more money too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rusty Dedrick interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Noah Evans
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Claude Thornhill and I was sent to him to write for the orchestra.

about_quotes.speaker: Chauncey Morehouse
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Claude Thornhill had his orchestra and you were a member of that.

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: Marshall would get those guys in to tutor the band. And we’d have doctor — he would take some stock arrangements and doctor them up and change them and then we’d have LaPorta would write original stuff for the band. So we are doing jazz festivals and we’re doing all the dances. ... along with Gil Ev
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003
